missionOverview.title: NASA's Psyche Mission
missionOverview.description: Psyche is a NASA mission to explore a unique metal-rich asteroid, also named Psyche, orbiting the Sun between Mars and Jupiter. The mission aims to help scientists understand the building blocks of planet formation by studying a body that may be the exposed core of an early planet.

funFacts.fact: The Magnetometer will detect and measure any remnant magnetic field on Psyche. If one exists, it would confirm the asteroid was once a planetary core.
The Multispectral Imager uses two cameras with color filters to capture images of Psyche's surface, helping scientists map its geology and mineral composition.
The Gamma-Ray and Neutron Spectrometer will determine what chemical elements make up Psyche's surface by detecting gamma rays and neutrons emitted from the asteroid.
Psyche also carries an experimental Deep Space Optical Communication (DSOC) system that uses lasers instead of radio waves to send data, achieving record-breaking data rates from deep space.
The Gravity Science experiment uses the spacecraft's radio system to measure Psyche's gravity field, which reveals clues about the asteroid's interior structure and composition.
